scope: Neovim/Vim plugin runtime and TUI runtime delivery
constraints: Hub remains central control plane and task owner.
Clients remain thin adapters that call backend APIs.
Approval, policy and mutation controls stay backend-enforced.
Complex admin/audit deep surfaces remain browser-first.

nvim_runtime.stack: Lua runtime plugin plus Vim command shim
reasoning: Neovim supports native Lua integration for maintainable command dispatch and rendering.
A Vim compatibility shim can be added incrementally without duplicating backend logic.

vim_compatibility.stack: Vimscript wrapper around shared backend command bridge
reasoning: Keeps command naming predictable for users.
Avoids full feature-parity pressure in first runtime milestone.

tui_runtime.stack: Python prompt_toolkit based app shell and views
reasoning: prompt_toolkit is already part of the repository dependency graph.
Supports keyboard-first terminal workflows and explicit rendering control.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Does not satisfy runnable client requirements.
High risk and unnecessary for additive migration from existing contracts.
